name: Goan-Style Chicken with Roasted Coconut (Shakoothi)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place coriander seeds, cumin seeds, mustard seeds, cinnamon, cloves, peppercorns, nutmeg, and red chilies in a small frying pan. Place over medium flame.
2. Dry-roast the spices, stirring frequently, until they emit a pleasant aroma.
3. Empty the spices into a clean coffee grinder or spice grinder and grind to a fine powder.
4. Put the ground spices in a bowl.
5. Add the grated coconut to the same frying pan and dry roast over medium flame, stirring constantly. The coconut should turn brown with roasted flecks and smell toasted.
6. Transfer the coconut and its aroma to the bowl with the ground spices.
7. Place garlic, ginger, and green chili into an electric blender with some water. Blend until a smooth paste is formed.
8. Heat oil in a 10-12 inch frying pan or saute pan over medium-high flame. When hot, add onions and fry until brown spots appear.
9. Pour the garlic-ginger mixture into the pan and stir once.
10. Lower heat to medium and add chicken pieces, salt, and the spice-coconut mixture to the bowl.
11. Stir and fry the chicken for 3-4 minutes or until it loses its pink color.
12. Reduce heat to low and cook for 25 to 30 minutes or until the chicken is tender. Stir a few times during this period, turning over each piece of chicken to ensure even coloring.
